title: Stacked bar chart showing issues by status category color
description:
Implement the stacked bar chart in the metrics panel. Bars are grouped by a chosen dimension (e.g. issue type or project) and each bar is segmented by status, with segments colored by the status category color from .kanbus.yml.

Scenario: Chart shows a bar per issue type

Given the metrics view is active
Then the chart displays one bar per issue type that has at least one issue in the current filtered view
And each bar is labeled with the issue type name

Scenario: Each bar segment is colored by status category

Given the chart is rendered
Then each segment within a bar represents a status
And the segment color matches the category color for that status: grey (Radix gray) for To Do, blue for In Progress, green for Done
And if a status has no category color a neutral fallback color is used

Scenario: Chart colors use the same Radix color tokens as the rest of the UI

Given the chart is rendered in light mode
Then chart segment colors use the Radix light-mode scale values for gray, blue, and green
Given the chart is rendered in dark mode
Then chart segment colors use the Radix dark-mode scale values

Scenario: Chart is responsive

Given the metrics panel is visible
Then the chart fills the available width of its container
And rescales when the panel or viewport is resized

Scenario: Chart reflects active filters

Given the user has filtered the board to show only epics from one project
When the metrics view is active
Then the chart only includes issues that match the current active filters
And the total count in the left column matches the data shown in the chart

Scenario: Chart has a legend

Given the chart is rendered
Then a legend is shown identifying which color corresponds to which status or status category
creator: ryan.porter
